reasoning: The FRED statistical model predicted a 29% chance of the price increasing past its last huge jump (from 3.015 to 3.502), assuming mean reversion. However, web search reveals a massive exogenous shock: the US and Israel have entered a war with Iran, severely disrupting Persian Gulf oil shipments and pushing crude past $100/barrel. According to a Forbes report from March 14, 2026, the national average gas price had already climbed to $3.67 due to this ongoing conflict. Given the phrasing of the question (comparing the latest available value on 03-22 to the latest available on 03-15), it is asking if the March 16 EIA print will exceed the March 9 print (3.502). Since ongoing tracking already places prices well above $3.50 in mid-March, and gas prices typically lag crude oil spikes ("rockets and feathers"), a continued increase is highly probable. I am heavily overriding the statistical prior due to the clear presence of a major, ongoing structural shock.
